ai_agent.role: Coordinates all utility services (water, sewer, electric, septic, gas) from availability verification through meter installation and account closing

staff.name: Dandara Souza
staff.role: Utilities Coordinator — also handles Inspections. Manages water/sewer/energy/septic for all projects, UAL requests, TUG side determination, account opening/closing

core_knowledge.description: 5 options from UAL: Standard (normal connection), Info Not Available (rare, payment to verify), Benefited/Benef (extension ready, extra cost → client hold), Extension (line extension needed, high cost → client hold), Well (no water access, install well). Each triggers different workflow.

4 options: Gravity (public system, rare in Marion), Septic (private, most common in Marion, no extra cost), Nitrogen (efficient septic, extra cost → client hold), LPS (Charlotte County system, mid-tier). System type determines documentation for Permits.
Side of the electrical box on the house. CICLE: always garage side. DUC/FPL/WEC: must ask utility company engineer. Critical info for Scheduling (RF Electric). Wrong side = significant extra cost.

Utility Availability Letter requested from MCU by email with address and parcel. Results distributed to: Drive folder, BT folder, BT Schedule fields, Utilities spreadsheet, Slack Permit channel, and Canva Red Flags for special cases.

Four companies with different processes: DUC (portal, long timelines, open at permit), FPL (portal, similar to DUC), CICLE/Recitrus (forms, signatures, open after 2nd draw), WEC (pay per connection). Each has unique underground electric and meter installation flows.

Triggered by Roof Assembly inspection approval. Steps: verify stakes, request service (Alisbird for conventional Marion, Howard for nitrogen Marion, different company for Citrus), manage connection (Alisbird does it free, Howard requires separate plumber Colano), DOH inspection, get owner signature via CR.

daily_workflows.name: New Jobsite Utility Setup
steps: Receive notification of new jobsite via Slack
Identify house, address, water/sewer/energy companies
Request UAL by email to water/sewer company
Register request date in Utilities spreadsheet
On receipt: analyze water availability (5 options)
Analyze sewer availability (4 options)
Register in Daily Logs with specific tags
Register in BT custom fields (companies and systems)
If extra cost: notify Slack for Client Relations → client approval → Financeiro
critical_decisions: Identifying extra costs that trigger client holds

daily_workflows.name: Energy Account and Underground Electric
steps: Receive permit notification via Slack
Identify energy company via interactive maps
Open account (varies by company)
Forward opening invoice to Financeiro
Determine TUG side and inform Scheduling
After TUG Inspection approval: notify company for underground electric scheduling
Schedule in Daily Logs with 3-week timeline
Verify invoice (>$1K over budget → client hold)
Coordinate electric meter installation

daily_workflows.name: Account Closing Post-Sale
steps: Receive closing notification
Verify accounts are in Lakeshore's name
Cancel energy (simple process)
Cancel water (requires document + signature)
critical_decisions: Timing of account cancellation vs owner transfer

handoffs.trigger: TUG side determined by energy company
handoffs.data_exchanged: TUG side (garage side vs other), company name, timeline
handoffs.impact: RF Electric installation depends on correct TUG side — wrong side = costly rework

handoffs.trigger: Water/sewer system type determined from UAL
Extra cost identified (extension, nitrogen, well, underground >$1K over budget)
handoffs.impact: Client approval required before proceeding — blocks pipeline

handoffs.trigger: Account opening invoice received or construction charges due
handoffs.data_exchanged: Invoice details, PO updates, payment requests
handoffs.impact: Payment delays can block meter installation and underground scheduling

pain_points.description: Triple registration required — same information entered in spreadsheet, Daily Logs, AND BT custom fields
pain_points.current_workaround: Manual entry in all three locations
pain_points.proposed_solution: Single entry point with automated sync to other systems

pain_points.description: Utility companies are difficult to work with — poor responsiveness, meters installed without notification or stolen
pain_points.current_workaround: Weekly field monitoring by data loggers after 1-month mark
pain_points.proposed_solution: Automated utility company status polling where APIs available

pain_points.description: Unpredictable timelines from utility companies, especially DUC (Duke Energy)
pain_points.current_workaround: Open DUC accounts immediately at permit receipt to buffer timeline
pain_points.proposed_solution: Historical timeline data to improve ETA predictions

pain_points.description: TUG side installed on wrong side causes significant additional cost
pain_points.current_workaround: Double-check with energy company engineer before communicating to Scheduling
pain_points.proposed_solution: Mandatory TUG side confirmation workflow with approval step

tribal_knowledge.description: Dandara knows the specific rules for each combination of county and utility company — different forms, contacts, timelines, and procedures. Each county (Marion, Citrus, Charlotte) has different companies with unique processes.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: New staff would need months to learn all county-company combinations; errors would cause delays

tribal_knowledge.description: Which installer to use based on county and type: Alisbird (Marion conventional, includes connection), Howard (Marion nitrogen, cheaper but no connection — need Colano plumber), different company for Citrus (all-in-one).
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: Wrong installer selection; missing connection step with Howard

training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: Water/sewer types, cost trigger thresholds, registration locations

topics: DUC vs FPL vs CICLE vs WEC processes
training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: Company-specific flows, TUG side rules by company, timeline expectations
Septic types, installer selection, inspection flow

compliance_requirements.requirement: UAL must be obtained before construction planning begins
compliance_requirements.verification: UAL document filed in Drive and BT per SOP

compliance_requirements.requirement: DOH approval required for septic systems before covering
compliance_requirements.regulation: Florida Department of Health septic regulations
compliance_requirements.verification: Septic permit saved in BuilderTrend after DOH inspection

compliance_requirements.requirement: Impact fees paid before Final Electric inspection
compliance_requirements.verification: Financeiro confirms payment; Dandara verifies before scheduling inspection

key_metrics.target: <5 business days from request to receipt

key_metrics.measurement: Zero rework incidents for TUG side errors

key_metrics.target: 100% of meters confirmed within 6 weeks of payment
